headline: GRAINS SLIP AHEAD OF WASDE, CATTLE HOLD GAINS
subheadline: Corn leads complex lower while livestock splits on screwworm clarity.
lead: Corn closed $4.15, down 4 cents and leading the grain complex lower ahead of this morning's WASDE report at 11:00 AM CT. The funds are positioning defensive with managed money net long still above 100,000 contracts, but yesterday's Brazil CONAB estimate adding 0.2% to their corn outlook removes any supply tightness narrative. Cattle held $242.07, up a dime, as screwworm confirmation paradoxically steadied the complex by removing uncertainty even as local management costs rise.
the_takeaway: Grains waiting on WASDE, cattle finding footing on clarity over chaos.
teaser: Corn slides 4 cents pre-WASDE while cattle steady on screwworm confirmation removing market uncertainty.

one_number.context: CONAB's latest projection shows Brazil's combined corn crop up 0.2% from May on better first-crop yields. That's another 280,000 tons of global supply hitting the books just as US corn waits for WASDE's domestic demand revision. The timing pressures US export pace when South American availability stays ample.

yesterdays_call.summary: Cattle bounce real but narrow, feeders lagging says recovery is selective.
yesterdays_call.note: Feeders flipped to leading higher at $356.30, confirming the recovery broadened past live cattle.

sections.title: CORN LEADS GRAINS LOWER
sections.body: Corn dropped 4 cents to $4.15, the biggest single-session decline in eight days, with December giving back 3¾ cents to $4.43. **Brazil's CONAB estimate lifted their corn outlook 0.2%** to 140.5 million tons, removing any supply tightness story the funds were banking on. Managed money is still net long over 100,000 contracts and this morning's WASDE at 11:00 AM CT will test whether domestic demand revisions can offset South American abundance. The chart shows corn testing the 200-day average again, same level that held two weeks ago.
sections.catalyst: CONAB raised Brazil corn estimate 0.2% to 140.5 million tons
sections.bottom_line: Funds getting defensive ahead of WASDE, Brazil supply weighing on outlook.
sections.vs_yesterday: Corn: broke from yesterday's tick higher into outright decline.

sections.title: CATTLE STEADY ON SCREWWORM CLARITY
sections.body: Live cattle added a dime to $242.07 while feeders gained $1.78 to $356.30, the strongest session for feeders in a week. **New World screwworm confirmation removed market uncertainty** even as local producers face higher management costs and movement restrictions. The paradox is classic cattle futures: clarity beats chaos, even when the clarity is negative. Feeders leading higher signals the market is pricing contained impact rather than widespread contagion. Processing capacity remains normal, keeping the fundamental supply pipeline intact.
sections.catalyst: New World screwworm confirmation removes market uncertainty
sections.bottom_line: Confirmation beats uncertainty; feeders leading says impact stays contained.
sections.vs_yesterday: Cattle: narrative held, feeders now leading the recovery as called.

sections.title: MILK FALLS SHARPLY ON OVERNIGHT SURPRISE
sections.body: Class III milk dropped $0.51 to $15.94, a 3.2% decline that registered as an overnight surprise after yesterday's rally. The move reverses half of Tuesday's gains and puts milk back below the $16 psychological level. **Wisconsin dairy checkoff lawsuit adds regulatory uncertainty** to a complex already dealing with processing capacity questions. The cheese block-barrel spread widened again, suggesting underlying demand pressure that spot prices aren't fully capturing. Dairy's volatility continues to reflect structural shifts the futures struggle to price cleanly.
sections.catalyst: Wisconsin dairy checkoff lawsuit adds regulatory uncertainty
sections.bottom_line: Overnight surprise erases half of Tuesday's gains, regulatory clouds building.

sections.title: ENERGY SLIPS ON HORMUZ TENSIONS
sections.body: WTI crude fell $2.04 to $89.79 while natural gas dropped 9 cents to $3.11, both registering their sharpest declines in over a week. **Trump's threat to seize Iran's Kharg Island oil hub escalated Hormuz tensions** even as Japan secured July crude volumes matching last year by diversifying suppliers. The Iran-Hormuz tensions, ongoing since early April, appear entering a new phase with direct threats to oil infrastructure. Chinese refiners are rotating away from Iranian crude, but the supply displacement is finding other buyers faster than the market expected.
sections.catalyst: Trump threatens to seize Iran's main oil export hub Kharg Island
sections.bottom_line: Hormuz escalation meets supply diversification; displacement working faster than expected.

outside_the_pit.title: Merck Animal Health acquires TARGAN poultry tech company
outside_the_pit.body: The biodevice solutions firm develops WingScan and other performance-monitoring tools for poultry operations. Consolidation in animal health tech reflects producers' growing demand for precision livestock management as margins tighten.

outside_the_pit.title: Trump names country music star John Rich as landowner envoy
outside_the_pit.body: Rich will oppose solar and wind projects on agricultural land. The appointment signals the administration's intent to prioritize food production over renewable energy development in rural America, potentially affecting land-use decisions nationwide.

outside_the_pit.title: House farm labor bill gains momentum despite deportation tensions
outside_the_pit.body: Rep. Dan Newhouse says immigration reform has a 'real shot' this Congress even as other members push deportation. The contradiction reflects agriculture's labor dependency versus broader political currents on immigration enforcement.

spread_to_watch.label: December corn / July corn calendar spread
spread_to_watch.level: 28 cents carry, narrowing from 31 cents Monday
spread_to_watch.commentary: The carry's compressing as funds position defensive ahead of WASDE. Normal seasonal would widen the spread into harvest, but Brazil's supply additions are pressuring new-crop more than old. Watch if WASDE demand revisions can flip this back to seasonal widening.

basis.headline: Eastern Belt corn basis firming on ethanol restart demand
basis.body: **Illinois River basis tightened 3 cents** as ethanol plants return from maintenance outages. Indiana and Ohio terminals are seeing steady farmer sales but merchandiser buying picked up pace. Western Belt staying soft, consistent with rail freight advantages favoring eastern origins into Gulf export.

weekly_thread.question: Will the grain complex break out of its five-day slide this week?
weekly_thread.status_text: The slide extended today with corn down 4 cents, the sharpest single-session decline yet. WASDE this morning is the test whether demand revisions can arrest the momentum or if South American supply abundance keeps the pressure on.

the_more_you_know.title: Why Brazil corn estimates move US futures faster than weather
the_more_you_know.body: Today's 0.2% CONAB revision to 140.5 million tons dropped corn 4 cents, more than most weather headlines manage. **Brazil produces 140 million tons against US corn's 385 million, but timing is everything.** Their safrinha harvest peaks just as US export programs need to compete globally. Every million tons of Brazilian supply shifts 15-20 million bushels of potential US export demand. The funds know South American abundance kills US basis premiums faster than domestic weather builds them.

watch_list.desc: USDA WASDE report; corn demand revisions above 14.6 billion bushels could arrest the slide
CBOT grain close; corn below $4.10 breaks to new session lows
Weekly export sales; corn shipments under 800K MT extends demand concerns into next week

daily_quote.text: You have power over your mind — not outside events. Realize this, and you will find strength.

source_summary: USDA, CME, Brazil CONAB, trade sources
